threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’ll know a newbie by how they still call it Dalston Junction instead of just Dalston.
Last night on the 59, a guy argued with the driver about the music. It was like a live DJ battle, but the driver wasn't having it and just cranked up the radio.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I heard a group of teens arguing over whether the best Döner is from Hasenheide or Hermannplatz. Their passion was real.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a guy in a panda suit directing traffic at a Kreuzberg intersection like it’s the most normal thing in the world.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid the BART after 5 PM on weekdays—it's a circus. Tourists think they can save time; we just sigh and walk.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo pull out a saxophone during a delay at Civic Center today. Just another Tuesday in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the Carrer Gran de Gràcia is a posh shopping avenue, but locals know it’s just a back route for avoiding the crowds at plaça de la Vila.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still struggle with the siesta—eating lunch at noon while everyone else is out like a light.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 10 minutes late is too late; everyone here shows up whenever. Time's a stretch, not a deadline.
Caught a lad singing to his reflection in the bus window near Brixton Academy. Didn’t know whether to clap or offer him a tissue.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a whole family of wild rabbits casually grazing in my garden while the neighbor played accordion, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the U-Bahn trains sometimes stop for a minute while the driver just chats with the station staff? Friends catching up mid-commute.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their casual disdain for the Muni delays—most non-locals blame the bus, while we just shrug and check our phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the excitement at Civic Center when a dog on the tracks turned the whole station into a live-action comedy. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think all the plazas are for mingling, but locals see them as quiet escape spots between errands, not social hubs.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a new arrival by the way they wait for the tram, checking the app repeatedly instead of enjoying a coffee at the stop.
